title: Upper Airway Lesions in the Neonate
authors: Otteson TD, Shah J / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Upper Airway Lesions in the Neonate?
Stridor ในทารกแรกเกิดไม่ใช่แค่ laryngomalacia — มี spectrum กว้างตั้งแต่จมูกไปถึง trachea ทุก level อาจเป็น life-threatening ได้; (1) Neonates เป็น obligate nasal breathers 4–6 สัปดาห์แรก — bilateral nasal obstruction (choanal atresia, pyriform stenosis) → immediate respiratory crisis; (2) Laryngomalacia คือ cause ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดของ stridor ในเด็กทารก (most common) แต่มี synchronous lesion 20–50% → ต้องตรวจทั้ง airway เสมอ; (3) Subglottic hemangioma มี propranolol เป็น first-line therapy ตั้งแต่ 2014 — เปลี่ยน management อย่างสิ้นเชิง; (4) CHARGE syndrome เกี่ยวข้องกับ choanal atresia + multilevel airway obstruction → มักต้องการ tracheostomy; (5) CPAP nasal prongs ทำให้เกิด nasal trauma ที่มีผลระยะยาวถึง adolescence — ป้องกันได้ด้วยการดูแล skin ที่ถูกต้อง. เรื่องนี้เกี่ยวข้องกับงาน NICU ทุกวัน เพราะเราคือคนที่ intubate, use CPAP, และต้องรู้ว่าเมื่อใดต้อง call ENT.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
แบ่ง neonatal upper airway เป็น 4 components และระบุ lesion ที่พบที่แต่ละระดับ
อธิบาย pyriform aperture stenosis: anatomy, การวินิจฉัยด้วย CT (< 11 mm), ความสัมพันธ์กับ holoprosencephaly, การรักษา conservative vs surgical
วินิจฉัยและ manage choanal atresia: incidence, CHARGE syndrome, การใส่ 6-French catheter, transnasal endoscopic repair, recurrence risk factors
อธิบาย congenital midline nasal masses: dermoid vs cerebral heterotopia vs encephalocele — ข้อควรระวัง biopsy, Furstenberg test
วินิจฉัยและ manage Pierre Robin sequence + micrognathia: conservative → MDO → tracheostomy criteria
อธิบาย laryngomalacia: etiology, ลักษณะ stridor, natural history (resolution 2 yr), dysphagia in 50%, supraglottoplasty indications
จำแนก vocal cord paralysis: unilateral vs bilateral — etiologies (Chiari malformation, cardiac, surgical), management algorithm
ใช้ Cohen classification สำหรับ laryngeal web (type 1–4) และ Cotton-Meyer grading สำหรับ subglottic stenosis (grade 1–4)
อธิบาย subglottic hemangioma: fluctuating biphasic stridor, GLUT-1 marker, propranolol 2 mg/kg/day protocol, PHACES association
อธิบาย Benjamin-Inglis classification ของ laryngeal cleft (type 1–4) และการรักษาแต่ละ type
อธิบาย CHAOS (Congenital High Airway Obstruction Syndrome): prenatal findings, EXIT procedure, Fraser syndrome association
ระบุ preventable factors ของ iatrogenic subglottic stenosis: tube size, cuffed tube, infection, movement — prevention in NICU
1. Anatomy ของ Neonatal Upper Airway — ความแตกต่างจากผู้ใหญ่
1.1 สี่ Components หลัก

แบ่ง neonatal upper airway เป็น 4 ส่วน: (1) Nasal, (2) Oral/Oropharyngeal, (3) Laryngeal, (4) Tracheal — obstruction ที่แต่ละ level มี clinical presentation ที่แตกต่างกัน ช่วยนำไปสู่ diagnosis ที่ถูกต้อง
1.2 ลักษณะพิเศษของ Neonatal Larynx
Obligate nasal breathers: 4–6 สัปดาห์แรก — lingual surface ชิด hard/soft palate → vacuum → respiratory distress ทันทีเมื่อมี bilateral nasal obstruction
Laryngeal position: birth = C4 → adult = C7; ตำแหน่งสูง → protection จาก external trauma และ ↓ aspiration risk ระหว่าง suckle
Epiglottis: rests on nasopharyngeal surface of soft palate ใน infant → allows simultaneous suckling + nasal breathing
Arytenoid cartilages: adult-sized at birth → large relative to supraglottic structures → contributes to laryngomalacia pathogenesis
Subglottis = narrowest point: (not glottis like adult) → subglottic stenosis risk post-intubation; normal diameter 5–7 mm term infant
1.3 Character of Stridor → Level of Obstruction

rows: Subglottic stenosis, hemangioma, vocal cord paralysis
Choanal atresia, pyriform stenosis, adenoid hypertrophy

blocks.text: 2. Nasal และ Nasopharyngeal Lesions
2.1 Pyriform Aperture Stenosis

Anatomy: pyriform aperture = bony inlet of nose (nasal bones superiorly + frontal process of maxilla laterally + premaxilla inferiorly); บาง overgrowth of medial maxillary process → unilateral or bilateral restriction
Presentation: tachypnea, difficulty passing NGT, apneic events, cyclic cyanosis improved with crying
Association: holoprosencephaly, absent maxillary frenulum, pituitary dysfunction, central maxillary megaincisor — ตรวจสอบ holoprosencephaly ทุกราย
Diagnosis: CT scan — pyriform aperture width < 11 mm ในทารกครบกำหนด = diagnostic; แต่ขนาด ≠ ความรุนแรงของอาการเสมอไป

Management: conservative first (topical decongestants + topical steroids + CPAP/McGovern nipple); prognosis excellent (aperture enlarges with craniofacial growth); surgical: nasal dilation/sublabial drill-out เมื่อมี sleep apnea, growth restriction, failure to extubate
2.2 Nasolacrimal Duct Cysts (Dacryocystoceles)
Congenital mucoceles: proximal + distal obstruction ของ nasolacrimal duct system
Clinical: cystic swelling lower medial canthus + epiphora + mucopurulent discharge + submucosal nasal cyst below inferior turbinate
Diagnosis: CT/MRI; conservative (decongestants, antibiotics, warm compress, massage); surgical: endoscopic marsupialization + dacryocystorhinostomy เมื่อมี infection, respiratory compromise
2.3 Choanal Atresia

Choanal Atresia — สิ่งที่ neonatologist ต้องรู้
Incidence: 1/5,000–7,000 live births; 2/3 unilateral (right side affected > left); females > males. Bilateral choanal atresia → symptoms always in neonatal period: distress at rest → ลดลงเมื่อร้อง (crying → forced mouth breathing); cyanosis with feeding. Unilateral: diagnosed later (chronic nasal obstruction/rhinorrhea). CHARGE syndrome ใน 50%: Coloboma, Heart defect, Atresia choanae, Restriction of growth, Genital hypoplasia, Ear anomalies; CHD7 gene (8q12) mutation ใน 90–95% ของ CHARGE criteria. Diagnosis: ไม่สามารถสอด 6-French catheter ผ่าน nasopharynx + flexible nasal endoscopy + CT (thickness + width ของ atretic plate). Management: bilateral → repair ภายใน first few days; transnasal endoscopic repair (predominant): Hopkins rod, urethral sound/microdrill → progressive dilation → backbiter removes posterior vomer. Stenting not recommended (↑ infection, synechiae). Mitomycin C increasingly abandoned. CHARGE patients: likely need tracheostomy upfront (multilevel obstruction); recurrence risk factors: reflux, age <10 days, pure bony atresia, associated malformations.
2.4 Congenital Midline Nasal Masses

3 types: (1) Dermoid (ecto + mesodermal tissue; sinus tracts, sebaceous secretion, hair at rhinion; enlarged foramen cecum/bifid septum → intracranial extension → recurrent meningitis risk); (2) Cerebral heterotopia (nasal glioma) (glial tissue sequestra; 15% retain dural connection; 10–30% retain fibrous stalk → incomplete resection risk; NOT truly a glioma); (3) Encephalocele (intracranial communication maintained; pulsatile, transilluminates; positive Furstenberg test — expansion with jugular vein compression)
RULE: ไม่ biopsy midline nasal mass โดยไม่มี CT + MRI ก่อน — encephalocele biopsy → CSF leak + meningitis + death
Treatment: complete surgical excision with neurosurgical collaboration as indicated; transnasal endoscopic approach gaining popularity
2.5 Nasopharyngeal Teratoma
Benign tumor: all 3 germ layers + early organ differentiation; females 6:1; associated fetal conditions: hydrops, pulmonary hypoplasia
AFP elevated: monitor for progression/treatment response
EXIT procedure: indicated ใน severe prenatal airway obstruction — evaluate ด้วย prenatal US + MRI + multidisciplinary care
2.6 CPAP Nasal Trauma
Nasal CPAP prongs/masks → nasal injury: cosmetic + functional sequelae documented ไม่เพียงแต่ใน neonates แต่ยัง adolescents + adults ที่มีประวัติ CPAP ตั้งแต่ทารก (Li et al JPRAS 2015)
Prevention: ถ้า nasal injury กำลังจะเกิดและไม่สามารถ discontinue CPAP → switch to nasal mask; nasal cannulas cause significantly less trauma than CPAP
Excessive suctioning → ↑ mucosal edema → exacerbation; rhinitis medicamentosa จาก prolonged decongestant use
3. Oral และ Oropharyngeal Lesions
3.1 Micrognathia และ Pierre Robin Sequence

Syndromes ที่เกี่ยวข้อง: Pierre Robin sequence, Treacher Collins, Goldenhar (oculoauriculovertebral dysplasia), Crouzon disease, Down syndrome — ทุก syndrome นี้มี micrognathia + glossoptosis → posterior airway obstruction
Natural history: normal growth → ↑ oropharyngeal space → ↓ obstructive symptoms; management ต้องคำนึงถึง growth potential

rows: Prone positioning + slight head elevation → dramatically ↓ obstruction
McGovern nipple (oral patency) or soft nasal trumpet until mandibular growth occurs
Multidisciplinary: pulmonology + genetics + swallow evaluation + PSG + airway eval + CT; MDO (mandibular distraction osteogenesis)
Craniofacial-microsomia-Goldenhar, persistent aspiration, poor neuromuscular tone, ventilator dependence, multilevel obstruction

blocks.text: Mandibular Distraction Osteogenesis (MDO): effective surgical technique to relieve airway obstruction from severe micrognathia; may obviate tracheostomy or allow decannulation (Lam et al JAMA Otolaryngol 2014)
3.2 Lymphatic Malformations
Benign congenital vascular anomalies; predilection for head and neck; floor of mouth/base of tongue → posterior tongue displacement → upper airway obstruction
Prenatal diagnosis + airway obstruction/floor-of-mouth involvement → EXIT procedure
Local infection → localized swelling → compromise existing airway; long-term antibiotics proposed for recurrent infections

Treatment: tailored to pathologic type (macrocystic vs microcystic); surgery + sclerotherapy = mainstay; sirolimus, propranolol, radiofrequency ablation have limited evidence; isolated neck disease = best prognosis
3.3 Oral Cavity Cysts
Lingual thyroglossal duct remnants (foramen cecum), dermoid cysts → progressive dysphagia/obstruction; tongue base cysts → acute airway crisis soon after birth or months later
Treatment: surgical excision; marsupialization for large unresectable cysts; temporary intubation สำหรับ significant tongue swelling post-op
4. Laryngeal Lesions
4.1 Laryngomalacia — Most Common Cause of Neonatal Stridor

Laryngomalacia — Clinical Summary
Most common cause of stridor in infant (most common laryngeal anomaly). Etiology: generalized neurologic immaturity of airway + digestive tract. Onset: stridor develops 2nd–4th week of life (occasionally at birth). Character: coarse, inspiratory; worse with agitation, supine; better with prone. Cry usually normal. Natural history: progressive until 8–12 months → complete resolution in most children by 2 years. Dysphagia/feeding difficulty: 50% of infants regardless of comorbidities → CSE/MBS/FEES indicated. GER co-exists frequently → treat with acid suppression. Synchronous airway lesion in 20–50% → direct laryngoscopy + rigid bronchoscopy in atypical patients. Severe laryngomalacia: constant stridor, ↑ WOB, apneas, FTT, cor pulmonale, pectus excavatum → supraglottoplasty: division of aryepiglottic folds + trimming redundant tissue (CO2 laser/cold instruments/microdebrider) → excellent success + improves concurrent OSA. Tracheostomy reserved for failure of supraglottoplasty.
4.2 Vocal Cord Paralysis — Second Most Common Laryngeal Anomaly
Bilateral: inspiratory stridor + ↑ with agitation/infection + weak voice + airway obstruction; associated neurologic abnormalities (Chiari malformation, hydrocephalus); often requires tracheostomy
Unilateral: may be initially asymptomatic; dysphonia + stridor worse with agitation; aspiration (choking/coughing with feeds); associated with cardiac atrial enlargement or anomalous great vessels

Workup: CXR + barium swallow/echo (cardiac); MRI brain/neck/chest (Chiari, mediastinal mass); awake fiberoptic laryngoscopy (confirm diagnosis + vocal cord motion); flexible bronchoscopy (subglottic airway)

Treatment unilateral: 20–40% remain symptomatic >8–12 months; conservative → temporary injection laryngoplasty → reinnervation (ansa cervicalis to RLN — ↑ voice + tone, not mobility, no foreign material)
4.3 Laryngeal Web — Cohen Classification

rows: Asymptomatic at birth; biphasic stridor 4–6 wk
Endoscopic; keel placement to prevent recurrence

blocks.text: Association: velocardiofacial syndrome (VCFS) / 22q11 deletion ใน anterior glottic webs → FISH หรือ genetic sequencing ทุกราย (Parkes Semin Fetal Neonat Med 2016)
Embryology: failure to recanalize laryngeal inlet ~10 weeks' gestation
4.4 Congenital Subglottic Stenosis — Cotton-Meyer Grading
Definition: subglottic diameter < 4 mm ใน term infant (normal 5–7 mm); elliptical or thickened cricoid ring
Associations: 22q11 deletion, Down syndrome, CHARGE syndrome — all have ↓ cricoid ring size
Cotton-Meyer grading (cuffless ETT → leak pressure 10–25 cmH2O): Grade 1 (0–50% obstruction) → Grade 2 (51–70%) → Grade 3 (71–99%) → Grade 4 (no discernible airway)
Mild: recurrent croup-like episodes 2–3 yr; severe: biphasic stridor with croupy cough from birth (no viral signs)
Management: anterior cricoid split (± graft) ใน infants ≤18 months; rib cartilage laryngotracheal reconstruction for severe/older; congenital form usually less severe than iatrogenic
4.5 Subglottic Hemangioma

Subglottic Hemangioma — Propranolol Era
Most common neoplasm of infant airway. GLUT-1 positive (distinguishes from other vascular lesions). ↑ prevalence: IVF, premature births, females. Typically located left posterior subglottis. Classic presentation: progressive biphasic stridor onset 4–6 weeks of age; symptoms FLUCTUATE day-to-day (vascular engorgement) — strongly diagnostic; worse with crying. Cry usually normal. PHACES association: segmental 'beard distribution' hemangioma + large cervicofacial hemangioma → screen for Posterior fossa anomalies, Hemangioma, Arterial lesions, Cardiac/aortic anomalies, Eye abnormalities, Sternal defects + further imaging. Natural history: proliferative phase 8–18 months → involutive phase 5–8 years. Treatment (first-line since FDA approval 2014): propranolol 2 mg/kg/day in 3 divided doses (Drolet Pediatrics 2013 consensus); monitor for hypotension, bradycardia, hypoglycemia. Nadolol 2.0–4.0 mg/kg/day: similar efficacy (Yang et al Int J Pediatr Otolaryngol 2021). Systemic steroids: controls proliferative phase in mild cases. Surgery/laser/tracheostomy: reserved for beta-blocker failure.
4.6 Laryngeal Cleft — Benjamin-Inglis Classification

rows: To level of true vocal cords

blocks.text: Etiology: failure of fusion of tracheoesophageal septum; associated syndromes: CHARGE, VACTERL, Optiz-Frias, Pallister Hall
Diagnosis: MBS/FEES + flexible laryngoscopy; definitive = palpation of interarytenoid area in OR (endoscopic diagnosis can miss type 1)
4.7 CHAOS — Congenital High Airway Obstruction Syndrome

Definition (Hedrick 1994): prenatally diagnosed upper airway obstruction + large echogenic lungs + dilated airways + flattened/inverted diaphragm + fetal ascites/hydrops — complete high airway obstruction → trapping of fetal lung secretions
Causes: laryngeal atresia, severe subglottic stenosis, glottic web, obstructive laryngeal cysts, double aortic arch (no TE connection)
Fraser syndrome association: laryngeal atresia + cryptophthalmos + syndactyly + renal agenesis + ear/genital abnormalities → detailed anomaly scan mandatory

Management: EXIT procedure (ex utero intrapartum treatment) — planned delivery maximizing survival; incomplete obstruction/TE fistula → less fluid trapping → less obvious on US; survival depends on degree of obstruction + ability to intubate/tracheostomy
5. Iatrogenic Laryngeal Injury — Intubation Trauma
5.1 Granuloma Formation
Post-extubation: hoarse voice → progressive (not improving) → yellow-red pedunculated mass at vocal process of arytenoid
Subglottic cysts/mucoceles: submucosal gland opening interrupted by ETT tissue reaction → cyst below vocal cords → often with concurrent subglottic stenosis
Treatment: microlaryngoscopy + removal; repeat evaluation 2–3 weeks (granuloma recurrence lessened by aggressive GER treatment)
5.2 Iatrogenic Subglottic Stenosis

Preventable Risk Factors ของ Iatrogenic Subglottic Stenosis — NICU Action List
Risk factors: prematurity (↓ cricoid ring size) + Down syndrome (↓ cricoid) + GER at time of intubation + infection + tube instability (repeated intubations) + extreme immaturity (months of intubation) + hypoxia + sepsis. Prevention steps: (1) Use smaller ETT; (2) Avoid cuffed ETT in infant/young child; (3) Aggressively treat systemic infection; (4) Minimize patient movement — prevent subglottic mucosal abrasion + accidental extubation (sedate as necessary); (5) Consider tracheostomy if prolonged intubation anticipated — neonates tolerate intubation much longer than children/adults; (6) Extubate under ideal conditions; high-dose systemic steroids 24–48h before + after extubation for difficult airway; inhaled epinephrine post-extubation for airway edema. Treatment: tracheostomy (cornerstone for multilevel/multisystem disease) → laryngotracheal reconstruction with rib cartilage augmentation; single-stage procedure possible in selected patients.
6. Algorithm — Approach to Neonatal Stridor
6.1 Emergency vs Stable Evaluation

ประเมิน degree of emergency ก่อน: air hunger, cyanosis, extreme tachypnea, ↓ consciousness → secure airway immediately (direct laryngoscopy + rigid bronchoscopy under GA); minimize manipulation จนกว่า airway จะ secured
Stable infant: flexible fiberoptic nasolaryngoscopy (dynamic laryngeal evaluation + vocal cord motion + degree of collapse during active respiration)

Ancillary imaging: CT neck with contrast (subglottic stenosis, soft tissue tumors, cysts); lateral/AP soft tissue neck XR (less common); airway fluoroscopy + MBS (vascular anomalies, TEF, swallowing disorders); MRI chest (mediastinal anatomy, vascular ring)
6.2 Differential Diagnosis by Character

rows: Bilateral nasal obstruction; worse at rest, better with crying
Coarse inspiratory stridor; onset 2–4 wk; better prone; fluctuates
Biphasic stridor; onset 4–6 wk; fluctuating severity day-to-day
Biphasic stridor + croupy cough; no viral signs

blocks.text: 7. NICU Practical — Clinical Pearls ในการ Manage Neonatal Airway
7.1 Approach เมื่อสงสัย Upper Airway Obstruction ใน NICU
ประเมิน 4 components: nasal → oral → laryngeal → tracheal; อย่า assume laryngomalacia โดยไม่ rule out levels อื่น
Bilateral nasal obstruction ในทารก < 4–6 สัปดาห์: emergency — ใส่ McGovern nipple/oral airway ทันที ขณะรอ ENT evaluation
การตรวจสอบ CHARGE syndrome: choanal atresia + heart defect + coloboma + growth restriction + genital hypoplasia + ear anomalies → genetic + CHD7 sequencing; plan tracheostomy upfront
Laryngomalacia + feeding difficulty in 50%: ทุกรายต้องได้ swallow evaluation (CSE/MBS/FEES) และ GER assessment + acid suppression
Subglottic hemangioma: ถาม propranolol อย่างแรก — ก่อนเริ่ม: ECG (HR, PR interval), BP, glucose; baseline echo ถ้า PHACES concern; ตรวจ glucose 1–2 h หลัง first dose
Prevent iatrogenic subglottic stenosis: uncuffed ETT + minimize movement + treat infection aggressively + GER treatment; ถ้า intubate > few weeks → plan ENT follow-up post-extubation
7.2 EXIT Procedure — รู้จักเบื้องต้น

EXIT (Ex Utero Intrapartum Treatment): fetus partially delivered via C-section; head/neck exposed; placental circulation maintained (uteroplacental gas exchange); airway secured (intubation or tracheostomy) before complete delivery
Indicated in: CHAOS, large cervicofacial teratoma/lymphatic malformation with impending airway obstruction diagnosed prenatally
Requires multidisciplinary team: OB + neonatology + pediatric surgery + ENT + anesthesia + OR setup simultaneously
8. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Neonates = Obligate Nasal Breathers: Bilateral Block = Emergency
ทารกแรกเกิดอายุ < 4–6 สัปดาห์เป็น obligate nasal breathers — tongue ชิด palate ตลอดเวลา → vacuum เมื่อจมูกอุดตัน → respiratory distress ทันที. Bilateral choanal atresia หรือ bilateral pyriform stenosis → cyanosis at rest ดีขึ้นเมื่อร้องไห้ (forced oral breathing). ทารกที่ 'cyanotic at rest + better when crying' = bilateral nasal obstruction until proven otherwise. First step: ใส่ McGovern nipple + call ENT; ถ้า bilateral choanal atresia + CHARGE: plan tracheostomy upfront เพราะ repair มักไม่สำเร็จในกลุ่มนี้

PEARL 2 — Laryngomalacia: 20–50% มี Synchronous Lesion ด้านล่าง
Laryngomalacia คือ diagnosis ที่พบบ่อยที่สุด แต่อย่า assume ว่า airway สะอาดด้านล่าง — synchronous airway lesion พบ 20–50% ของรายที่ severe. ดังนั้น infants ที่มี atypical features (biphasic stridor, cyanosis, feeding failure, persistent despite conservative treatment) → direct laryngoscopy + rigid bronchoscopy เพื่อ rule out subglottic stenosis, hemangioma, tracheomalacia ที่ซ่อนอยู่. Dysphagia และ feeding problems เกิดขึ้นใน 50% ของ laryngomalacia → ทุกรายต้องมี swallow evaluation เสมอ

PEARL 3 — Subglottic Hemangioma: 'Fluctuating Biphasic Stridor' = Key Clue
Subglottic hemangioma มี clinical signature ที่ distinctive: (1) onset 4–6 สัปดาห์; (2) biphasic stridor; (3) severity fluctuates day-to-day (vascular engorgement ↔ relaxation) — ต่างจาก subglottic stenosis ที่ static. ราย female + segmental 'beard' hemangioma ที่ head/neck → screen for PHACES. Treatment: propranolol 2 mg/kg/day (FDA approved 2014, Drolet Pediatrics 2013 consensus) — ก่อนเริ่มต้อง rule out hypotension, bradycardia, hypoglycemia, PHACES (structural cardiac lesion อาจ contraindicate propranolol).

PEARL 4 — Laryngeal Cleft Type 1: ต้อง Palpate in OR เพื่อวินิจฉัย
Laryngeal cleft type 1 (posterior interarytenoid area to vocal cord level) ทำให้เกิด recurrent aspiration + pneumonia ซ้ำๆ แต่ laryngoscopy ดูเผินๆ อาจปกติ. Definitive diagnosis ต้อง palpation ของ interarytenoid area in OR — ถ้าไม่ใส่ finger ลงไปตรวจ จะ miss. ดังนั้น infant ที่มี aspiration pneumonia ซ้ำ + laryngoscopy ดู 'ปกติ' → ต้องส่ง OR สำหรับ formal evaluation. Type 1: ⅓ resolves with thickened feeds + antireflux; ⅔ ต้องการ endoscopic surgical repair.

PEARL 5 — Midline Nasal Mass: ไม่ Biopsy ก่อนมี Imaging ครบ
Congenital midline nasal mass ระหว่าง columella และ glabella — อย่า biopsy โดยไม่มี CT + MRI ก่อน. Encephalocele มี intracranial connection + contains brain tissue/dura/CSF: biopsy → CSF leak + meningitis + death. ก่อน biopsy ต้องทำ CT (skull base involvement) + MRI (intracranial extension). ทดสอบ encephalocele ด้วย Furstenberg test (กด jugular vein → hemangioma expands) + transillumination. Cerebral heterotopia (nasal 'glioma') ≠ true glioma — เป็นเพียง glial tissue sequestrum; ตรวจสอบว่ามี fibrous stalk อยู่หรือไม่ก่อน excision เพื่อป้องกัน incomplete resection.
9. Evidence Update — Key Studies

Hedrick MH et al J Pediatr Surg 1994;29(2):271–274: first defined CHAOS (Congenital High Airway Obstruction Syndrome): prenatal echogenic lungs + dilated airways + inverted diaphragm + hydrops; systematic description + EXIT procedure concept

Drolet BA et al Pediatrics 2013;131:128: consensus statement on propranolol initiation for infantile hemangioma: 2 mg/kg/day divided 3 doses; screening + monitoring protocol for hypotension, bradycardia, hypoglycemia; foundational guideline for subglottic hemangioma management

Yang W et al Int J Pediatr Otolaryngol 2021;144:110688: nadolol 2.0–4.0 mg/kg/day vs propranolol for subglottic hemangioma — similar efficacy and side-effect profile; nadolol as viable alternative, especially with bronchospasm concerns

Kwong K Front Pediatr 2015;3:52: current updates on choanal atresia: transnasal endoscopic repair is gold standard; stenting not beneficial (↑ granulation, infection, synechiae); CHARGE patients need tracheostomy; recurrence risk factors

Hsu P et al J Paediatr Child Health 2014;50:504: CHARGE syndrome review: CHD7 gene (8q12) mutation in 90–95%; clinical diagnosis criteria; multidisciplinary management requirement for choanal atresia in CHARGE context

Lam DJ et al JAMA Otolaryngol Head Neck Surg 2014;140:338: outcomes of mandibular distraction osteogenesis for severe micrognathia: effective airway relief, ↓ tracheostomy need, ↑ decannulation rates; MDO preferred over tracheostomy when feasible

Leonard JA, Reilly BK NeoReviews 2021;22:e653: laryngomalacia in premature neonate: higher rates of feeding difficulties + GER; supraglottoplasty outcomes in preterm population; dysphagia evaluation essential

Hartl TT, Chadha NK Otolaryngol Head Neck Surg 2012;147:619: systematic review: laryngomalacia + acid reflux co-exist frequently; GER treatment improves laryngomalacia symptoms; acid suppression recommended

Bedwell J, Zalzal G Semin Pediatr Surg 2016;25:119: laryngomalacia review: synchronous airway lesion 20–50%; indications for supraglottoplasty (apnea, FTT, cyanosis, aspiration); complete resolution by 2 yr in most

Farhood Z et al JAMA Otolaryngol Head Neck Surg 2016;142:665: meta-analysis: supraglottoplasty → objective improvement in laryngomalacia + concurrent OSA; AHI ↓ significantly; supports surgical intervention for severe laryngomalacia with OSA
Cohen SR Ann Otol Rhinol Laryngol 1985;121:2: congenital glottic webs classification system (type 1–4 by glottic length involvement + subglottic extension); still in use as reference classification

Parkes WJ, Propst EJ Semin Fetal Neonat Med 2016;21:270: advances in neonatal laryngeal disorders: VCFS/22q11 association with anterior glottic webs; Cotton-Meyer grading update; modern endoscopic techniques for laryngeal web + stenosis
Benjamin B, Inglis A Ann Otol Rhinol Laryngol 1989;98:417: minor congenital laryngeal clefts diagnosis + classification (Benjamin-Inglis type 1–4); foundational classification still in clinical use

Jabbour J et al JAMA Otolaryngol Head Neck Surg 2014;140:428: pediatric vocal fold immobility natural history: 20–40% remain symptomatic >8–12 months; long-term follow-up necessary; timing of surgical intervention for unilateral paralysis

Butskiy O et al JAMA Otolaryngol Head Neck Surg 2015;141:654: systematic review of surgical interventions for pediatric unilateral VCP: reinnervation (ansa cervicalis to RLN) → ↑ voice + tone without foreign material; better long-term outcomes than injection alone

Li Y et al J Plast Reconstr Aesthet Surg 2015;68:339: late presenting nasal deformities after neonatal CPAP injury — 33-year experience; cosmetic + functional sequelae in adolescence; justification for careful CPAP skin care protocols

Nolan HR et al J Pediatr Surg 2019;54:1153: CHAOS natural history + prenatal management at single comprehensive fetal center: EXIT procedure planning; survival outcomes stratified by degree of obstruction

Jefferson ND et al Semin Pediatr Surg 2016;25:138: subglottic stenosis review: risk factors (GER, infection, tube size, movement); prevention strategies in NICU; laryngotracheal reconstruction outcomes with rib cartilage grafts
10. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Neonate อายุ 3 วัน มี cyanosis ขณะหายใจ พอร้องไห้อาการดีขึ้น — diagnosis ที่ต้องนึกถึงก่อนและ first step ในการวินิจฉัย?

ตอบ: Bilateral nasal obstruction (choanal atresia, pyriform aperture stenosis) — neonates เป็น obligate nasal breathers; crying = forced mouth breathing → cyanosis better. First step: attempt to pass 6-French catheter through each nostril into nasopharynx — failure to pass confirms obstruction. Then: flexible nasal endoscopy + CT scan (atretic plate thickness/width). Immediate: McGovern nipple/oral airway, call ENT; check for CHARGE syndrome (coloboma, heart, genital anomalies, ear) → CHD7 sequencing.
Q: อธิบาย CHARGE syndrome: criteria, gene, ความสำคัญต่อ choanal atresia management

ตอบ: CHARGE = clinical diagnosis: Coloboma (or ophthalmic anomalies) + Heart defect + Atresia choanae + Restriction of growth/development + Genital hypoplasia + Ear anomalies ± hearing loss. CHD7 gene (chromosome 8q12): mutation in 90–95% of CHARGE criteria. Management impact: CHARGE patients → more likely to require upfront tracheostomy (multilevel obstruction + poor pharyngeal muscular control + cardiopulmonary issues); choanal repair less likely to succeed (contracted anatomy); complete evaluation for associated anomalies mandatory in ALL bilateral choanal atresia.
Q: Laryngomalacia: natural history, สัดส่วนที่มี feeding problems, synchronous lesion, surgical indications

ตอบ: Natural history: stridor onset 2nd–4th week; progressive until 8–12 months → complete resolution in most children by 2 years. Feeding difficulty/dysphagia: 50% of infants regardless of comorbidities → CSE/MBS/FEES + GER treatment. Synchronous airway lesion: 20–50% → direct laryngoscopy + rigid bronchoscopy in atypical patients. Surgery (supraglottoplasty) when: cyanosis, apnea, FTT, aspiration, constant stridor not responsive to conservative therapy; tracheostomy only for failure of supraglottoplasty.
Q: Subglottic hemangioma vs subglottic stenosis: อะไรที่ช่วย differentiate ทั้งสองอย่าง?

ตอบ: Both: biphasic stridor; onset 4–6 weeks; cry usually normal. Key distinguishing feature of hemangioma: symptoms FLUCTUATE day-to-day (vascular engorgement with crying/agitation → variable severity) — subglottic stenosis is static. Other hemangioma clues: female sex, IVF, premature birth; segmental 'beard distribution' hemangioma → PHACES screen; GLUT-1+ on biopsy. Imaging: CT — hemangioma: asymmetric eccentric mass (usually left posterior); stenosis: concentric symmetric narrowing. Definitive: direct laryngoscopy + rigid bronchoscopy.
Q: Propranolol สำหรับ subglottic hemangioma: dose, monitoring, contraindications

ตอบ: Dose: 2 mg/kg/day in 3 divided doses (Drolet Pediatrics 2013 consensus); titrate based on side effects. Monitor: hypotension, bradycardia, hypoglycemia (check glucose 1–2h after first dose). Pre-treatment screening: ECG (HR, PR interval, QT), BP, glucose; echo if PHACES concern (structural cardiac lesion/aortic coarctation may contraindicate propranolol). Alternative: nadolol 2.0–4.0 mg/kg/day (Yang 2021) — similar efficacy; useful ใน bronchospasm risk. Duration: continue through proliferative phase (8–18 months); regression follows 5–8 years.
Q: Cohen classification ของ laryngeal web type 1–4 และ treatment ที่ต่างกัน

ตอบ: Type 1 (<35%): asymptomatic at birth; stridor 4–6 wk; endoscopic excision ± stent/keel. Type 2 (35–50%): respiratory distress; endoscopic + keel. Type 3 (50–75%): respiratory distress + aphonia; tracheostomy + open approach + cartilage graft. Type 4 (up to 99% + subglottic stenosis): severe; tracheostomy + laryngotracheal reconstruction. VCFS/22q11 association: anterior glottic webs → always do FISH/genetic sequencing for 22q11. Embryology: failure to recanalize laryngeal inlet ~GW 10.
Q: Cotton-Meyer grading ของ subglottic stenosis: grade 1–4 และวิธีตรวจ

ตอบ: Method: direct laryngoscopy + rigid bronchoscopy under GA; cuffless ETT สอดผ่าน stenosis → largest size ที่ให้ leak pressure 10–25 cmH2O → ใช้ size นั้นกำหนด grade. Grade 1: 0–50% obstruction; Grade 2: 51–70%; Grade 3: 71–99%; Grade 4: no discernible airway. Management: congenital (usually less severe) → anterior cricoid split (± graft) ≤18 months; rib cartilage LTR for grade 3–4 or older child. Associations: 22q11 deletion, Down syndrome, CHARGE.
Q: Laryngeal cleft: วิธีวินิจฉัยที่ definitive และ type ที่ conservative treatment ได้บ้าง

ตอบ: Definitive diagnosis: palpation of interarytenoid area in OR under GA — laryngoscopy + flexible scope อาจ miss type 1. Workup: MBS/FEES + flexible laryngoscopy ก่อน OR. Type 1 (to true vocal cords): ⅓ managed conservatively (thickened feeds + speech-language pathology + antireflux); ⅔ need endoscopic repair. Type 2–3: endoscopic repair (de-epithelialize + Vicryl sutures inferior → superior). Type 4: open approach; may need CPB/ECMO. Associated syndromes: CHARGE, VACTERL, Optiz-Frias, Pallister Hall.
Q: CHAOS: prenatal findings, pathophysiology, และ management

ตอบ: Prenatal findings: large echogenic lungs + dilated airways + flattened/inverted diaphragm + fetal ascites/nonimmune hydrops — ALL findings present only with COMPLETE obstruction (no TE connection). Pathophysiology: complete airway obstruction → fetal lung secretions trapped → lungs hyperexpand → esophageal compression → polyhydramnios (impaired amniotic fluid swallowing). Causes: laryngeal atresia, severe subglottic stenosis, glottic web, obstructive cysts, double aortic arch. Fraser syndrome association. Management: EXIT procedure planned at delivery — secure airway while placental circulation maintained.
Q: ระบุ risk factors ของ iatrogenic subglottic stenosis และ preventive steps ที่ทำได้ใน NICU

ตอบ: Risk factors: prematurity + Down syndrome (↓ cricoid size); cuffed ETT; GER + infection at intubation; tube instability (repeated intubations); extreme immaturity (months intubated); hypoxia + sepsis. Prevention: (1) uncuffed ETT; (2) smallest appropriate ETT size; (3) treat infection aggressively; (4) minimize patient movement (sedate as needed); (5) consider tracheostomy if prolonged intubation anticipated; (6) extubate under ideal conditions; (7) high-dose steroids 24–48h pre/post extubation for difficult airway; (8) inhaled epinephrine post-extubation for edema.
Q: Midline nasal mass: dermoid vs cerebral heterotopia vs encephalocele — อะไรที่บอกความแตกต่าง?

ตอบ: Dermoid: ecto + mesodermal tissue; sinus tracts with sebaceous material + hair at rhinion; ± recurrent meningitis (intracranial connection); enlarged foramen cecum/bifid nasal septum/bifid crista galli on imaging. Cerebral heterotopia (nasal 'glioma'): glial tissue sequestrum; 15% retain dural connection; 10–30% fibrous stalk → incomplete resection risk if missed; NOT a true neoplasm. Encephalocele: pulsatile + transilluminates + positive Furstenberg test (jugular vein compression → expansion); contains brain/dura/CSF; subarachnoid space communication maintained. Rule: CT + MRI BEFORE any biopsy — encephalocele biopsy → CSF leak + meningitis + death.

Day 167 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.70
บทต่อไป: Chapter 70 — Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia. เตรียมตัว: definition evolution (Northway 1967 → NIH 2001 → Jensen 2019 consensus) — severity grading ใหม่ที่ include respiratory support level; pathogenesis: arrested alveolar development (new BPD) vs oxygen toxicity + barotrauma (old BPD); risk factors: prematurity + mechanical ventilation + O2 + infection + PDA + nutrition; prevention strategies: antenatal steroids, surfactant, gentle ventilation, caffeine, vitamin A; long-term outcomes: pulmonary hypertension, neurodevelopment, exercise intolerance into adulthood.
